w:r.w:t: L&D Training Effectiveness Report - AI Agent & LLM Application Development
Audience: L&D Manager | Evidence: Passed | Status: Draft
Training effectiveness is strong with validated evidence.

The selected scope contains 1 course(s), 1 completed course(s), and 13 trainee-course records. Overall attendance is 90.4%, completion is 100%, pass rate is 92.3%, and average score is 7.88/10. Total training hours are 188 and scaled cost is 9.6. Overall effectiveness classification is Risk. Management interpretation: The training course 'AI Agent & LLM Application Development' has been completed with a total of 13 trainees. The overall attendance rate is 90.4%, and the completion rate is 100%, indicating strong engagement and fulfillment of course requirements. The pass rate stands at 92.3%, with an average score of 7.88 out of 10, reflecting satisfactory performance. However, the overall effectiveness classification is marked as Risk, suggesting potential areas for improvement despite the positive metrics.
Final conclusion is allowed. Overall classification is Risk.
Completion is based on attendance >= 70%, separate from pass rate.
Assessment achievement against course pass threshold.
Average assessment score from validated records.
Combined trainer and content feedback rating.
Scaled cost signal for relative comparison.
Proxy metric using post-training performance delta and scaled cost.
Validated performance metrics compared with L&D operating thresholds.

wp:docPr.name: overall-kpis
wp:docPr.descr: Validated performance metrics compared with L&D operating thresholds.
wp:docPr.title: Overall KPI vs target

w:r.w:t: NORM rule triggers grouped by business priority.

wp:docPr.name: norm-flags
wp:docPr.descr: NORM rule triggers grouped by business priority.
wp:docPr.title: Governance flags by priority

w:r.w:t: Evidence: Completion is calculated as attendance >= 70%; pass rate is tracked separately at 92.3%.
Implication: Learners are completing the required session exposure, and assessment achievement is also healthy.
Evidence: Attendance 90.4%, pass rate 92.3%, average score 7.88/10.
Implication: The selected scope is classified as Risk.
AI Agent & LLM Application Development has an effectiveness score of 89.47 in the selected scope. (Good)
Evidence: Supported by validated metrics, evidence gate, and L&D NORM checks.
Implication: Use this signal to prioritize follow-up actions and stakeholder communication.
2 outstanding trainees met the star learner rule: score >= 9.0 and full attendance. (Good)
Evidence: Supported by validated metrics, evidence gate, and L&D NORM checks.
Implication: Use this signal to prioritize follow-up actions and stakeholder communication.
1 high-priority L&D NORM flag was triggered. (Monitor)
Evidence: Supported by validated metrics, evidence gate, and L&D NORM checks.
Implication: Use this signal to prioritize follow-up actions and stakeholder communication.
Nevertheless, the classification of Risk indicates that there are underlying issues that need to be addressed, (Monitor)
Evidence: Supported by validated metrics, evidence gate, and L&D NORM checks.
Implication: Use this signal to prioritize follow-up actions and stakeholder communication.
Flag for coaching; assign buddy or practice resources
Improve learner recovery and reduce support-needed cases.
Assign a coaching owner, buddy support, and targeted practice resources for the flagged learner.
Improve learner recovery and reduce support-needed cases.
Continuous monitoring of trainee performance and feedback should be prioritized to ensure that all participants can achieve their learning objectives.
Required sheets and columns were mapped from Teams, OneDrive, Forms, KPI, Rules and Template sources.
Expected attendance rows are trainee count x total sessions. Late is retained for metrics as partial attendance.
Completed courses require one valid assessment and pass-status row per trainee.
NORM-14 requires at least 60% feedback response rate for valid analysis.
Duplicate rows and numeric ranges were checked before metric generation.
Consistency: course ID and pass-status logic
Cross-source course IDs and pass-status logic were checked against the selected course catalog.
Sensitive details: Visible; raw appendix: Allowed
Flag for coaching; assign buddy or practice resources
OpenAI gpt-4o-mini enriched narrative fields. Numeric metrics remain deterministic.
